entity_candidates.definition: Abbeyleix is a town in County Laois, Ireland, located about 14 kilometres south of Portlaoise. It lies in a civil parish of the same name and was historically on the N8 road, now bypassed by the M8 motorway. In 2023, Abbeyleix was named Ireland's Tidiest Town.

entity_candidates.definition: County Laois is a county in Ireland, part of the Eastern and Midland Region and the province of Leinster. Historically known as Queen's County and County Leix, its local authority is Laois County Council based in Portlaoise. As of the 2022 census, its population was 91,657.

relation_candidates.definition: Leinster is one of the four provinces of Ireland, located in the east of the island. It historically comprises the ancient kingdoms of Meath, Leinster, and Osraige, and has a 2022 population of 2,858,501. The province is an officially recognised subdivision of Ireland, listed on ISO 3166‑2 as IE‑L, and its flag features a golden harp on a green background.
